title: centaurea madrensis
intro: Centaurea madrensis, also known as the Madren Cornflower, is a rare, endangered plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Centaurea madrensis include its potential to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as its antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. Centaurea madrensis is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its availability and use are limited due to its endangered status.
